content:
Key Findings

  • Burnley Borough Council spent £355.2M across 30,580 transactions from 2021/22 to 2025/26
  • 100.0% of the council's payments were categorised as 'Unknown'
  • The council made an average of 6,116 transactions per year during this period
  • No specific payment types were disclosed in the published spending data

Understanding Burnley's Payment Categories

Burnley Borough Council's published spending data reveals that all £355.2M of transactions from 2021/22 to 2025/26 were categorised as 'Unknown'. This lack of categorisation makes it challenging to analyse the types of payments the council is making. The absence of specific payment types in the data suggests there may be opportunities to improve transparency in how the council reports its financial transactions.

The council made an average of 6,116 transactions per year during this five-year period. While the volume of transactions provides some insight into the council's financial activity, the lack of payment type information limits the ability to understand how these funds are being allocated across different services and suppliers.

Comparing Payment Methods

Without specific payment types disclosed, it is not possible to compare different methods of payment used by Burnley Borough Council. Typically, councils might use various payment methods such as bank transfers, cheques, or electronic payments. However, the data does not provide this level of detail. This lack of information makes it difficult to assess whether the council is utilising efficient and cost-effective payment methods.

The absence of payment type information also hinders the ability to identify any potential areas for improvement in the council's financial processes. For example, if a significant portion of payments were made via cheque, this might indicate an area where the council could transition to more modern and efficient electronic payment methods.

What You Can Do

  • Submit a Freedom of Information request to Burnley Borough Council asking for a breakdown of payment types used for the transactions categorised as 'Unknown'
  • Attend council committee meetings to ask questions about the council's payment methods and transparency practices
  • Check the council's published contracts register to see if more detailed information about payment types is available there
  • Write to your local councillor expressing your concerns about the lack of transparency in Burnley Borough Council's payment types and requesting more detailed reporting
  • Visit aidoge.co.uk to explore the full spending data and identify specific transactions you may want to investigate further
This article is based on Burnley Borough Council's published spending data covering 2021/22 to 2025/26. All figures are drawn directly from transactions published under the Local Government Transparency Code. Analysis by AI DOGE.
